title: Bridge Essay: Recent social stress and severity of auditory hallucinations.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on acontinuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**Recent social stress and severity of auditory hallucinations.**. Schizophr Res.. PMID: 38733801. DOI: 10.1016/j.schres.2024.04.024. BACKGROUND AND HYPOTHESIS: Hearing voices is acommon and often distressing experience for people with psychosis, and many individuals experience medication-resistant auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH). Psychosocial interventions are often employed to address distress over hearing voices. However, although links have been made between adverse social experiences and psychosis broadly, no work has yet delineated the relationship between day-to-day social stress and hallucination severity.
